description: Prioritized topics for content development, ranked by user demand, business value, expertise demonstration, and AI citation potential.

scoringMethodology.userDemand: 25% — Search volume + consumer pain point frequency
scoringMethodology.expertiseDemonstration: 25% — Ability to showcase unique expertise and data
scoringMethodology.aiCitationPotential: 20% — Likelihood AI engines will cite our answer
scoringMethodology.contentGap: 10% — How poorly this question is currently answered online

priorityTopics.topic: AI Contract Review — Core Pillar Consolidation
priorityTopics.rationale: Highest-impact single improvement. Three pages currently compete for this entity. Consolidating to one definitive pillar with merged best content will immediately boost entity authority from 55→80+.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Very high — 'AI contract review' is the most searched entity
priorityTopics.businessValue: Core platform offering — AI contract review is the flagship tool
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Strong — we have actual AI analysis methodology to explain
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Very high — direct answer to 'how does AI review contracts' is high-demand for AI engines
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — some decent answers exist but none authoritative

priorityTopics.topic: AI Bill Analysis — Strengthen Examples & ROI Data
priorityTopics.rationale: Already our strongest entity (80/100). Adding more bill-type-specific examples and an interactive savings calculator will make this the definitive consumer bill analysis resource.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Very high — 'check my bills for errors' is a universal consumer need
priorityTopics.businessValue: High — bill analysis is the most personally relevant tool for consumers
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Very strong — our existing examples with dollar amounts are already best-in-class
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Very high — 'how much can I save' questions have poor answers online
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — examples are good but calculators are missing

priorityTopics.topic: Hidden Fee Detection — Entity Definition & Industry Authority
priorityTopics.rationale: Entity lacks a canonical definition page. Creating a strong 'What Is Hidden Fee Detection?' entity page + expanding industry guides with 2026 data will establish this as the go-to resource.
priorityTopics.userDemand: High — 'hidden fees in [industry]' is evergreen consumer search behavior
priorityTopics.businessValue: Core mission — the platform is named after this concept
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Strong — we have industry-specific guides with real examples
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: High — AI engines need an authoritative source for 'what are hidden fees'
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — good industry guides exist but no canonical entity definition

priorityTopics.rationale: Two competing page versions (v1 legacy + v2). Consolidating into a single v2 page with specific use cases and savings examples will establish this entity properly.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Medium-high — 'can AI help me with finances' is growing as AI assistants become mainstream
priorityTopics.businessValue: Medium — advisory content supports tool usage
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Medium — we can explain the AI methodology but need more use-case examples
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Medium — AI engines need trusted sources for 'is AI financial advice reliable'
priorityTopics.contentGap: Large — most online answers are either overly promotional or dismissive

priorityTopics.topic: AI Contract Review vs. Lawyer — Authoritative Comparison
priorityTopics.rationale: This is the highest-priority comparison page (scored 92 in questions.json). Updated with 2026 cost data, accuracy benchmarks, and specific use-case recommendations, this page can become the definitive answer to 'should I use AI or hire a lawyer?'
priorityTopics.userDemand: Very high — every consumer considering AI contract review asks this question
priorityTopics.businessValue: High — directly addresses the #1 consumer objection to AI tools
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: High — we can provide specific cost comparisons and accuracy data
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Very high — AI engines frequently need to answer 'AI vs human' comparison questions
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — some comparisons exist but lack specific data

priorityTopics.topic: Consumer Financial Protection — Pre-Signing Checklist Authority
priorityTopics.rationale: The 'what should I check before signing' question has very high consumer demand. Merging the two checklist pages into one comprehensive, printable resource with industry-specific variants creates a linkable authoritative asset.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Very high — every contract signer wants a pre-signing checklist
priorityTopics.businessValue: High — checklists are highly shareable and drive engagement
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Medium — we can compile comprehensive checklists from our industry knowledge
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Medium — checklist formats are harder for AI to cite than narrative answers
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — many checklists exist but few are comprehensive and industry-specific

priorityTopics.rationale: Consumer trust in AI depends on transparency about accuracy and limitations. A comprehensive methodology page with testing data, accuracy rates by document type, and honest limitations builds E-E-A-T that supports all other pages.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Medium — 'is AI accurate' is asked by skeptical consumers before trying tools
priorityTopics.businessValue: Medium — trust content supports conversion on tool pages
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Very high — transparency about methodology demonstrates real expertise
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Medium — AI engines value transparent methodology for trust assessment
priorityTopics.contentGap: Large — most AI tools don't publish accuracy data or methodology

priorityTopics.topic: AI Fee Detector — How It Works (AEO Optimization)
priorityTopics.rationale: The 'how does AI detect hidden fees' question is the top AEO opportunity (scored 90 in questions.json). A clear, plain-language explanation of OCR, ML pattern matching, and market comparison will position us as the definitive source on AI fee detection mechanics.
priorityTopics.userDemand: High — consumers want to understand the technology before trusting it
priorityTopics.businessValue: High — directly explains the core platform technology
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Very high — we can explain our actual methodology in accessible terms
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Very high — AI engines need a clear 'how AI detects fees' answer
priorityTopics.contentGap: Large — no existing page explains this clearly in consumer-friendly language

priorityTopics.rationale: Construction contracts have the highest dollar amounts at stake for consumers. Strengthening this entity with change order analysis, material cost benchmarks, and contractor fee comparison data makes this the definitive construction contract resource.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Medium-high — homeowners searching for construction contract help have high intent
priorityTopics.businessValue: Medium — construction is one of several verticals
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Medium — we have industry-specific content but could add more data
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Medium — specific enough for AI to cite for construction fee questions
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — some construction contract resources exist but lack AI-specific guidance

priorityTopics.rationale: The 'agreement analyzer' entity is the blurriest in our entity model. Clarifying what makes an agreement different from a contract, and providing agreement-type-specific examples, will sharpen this entity for AI recognition.
priorityTopics.userDemand: Medium — users search for specific agreement types (lease, employment) more than 'agreement analyzer'
priorityTopics.businessValue: Medium — covers important use cases (employment, lease, freelance)
priorityTopics.expertiseDemonstration: Medium — we can explain what AI looks for in different agreement types
priorityTopics.aiCitationPotential: Medium — 'can AI review my [specific agreement type]' is a growing query pattern
priorityTopics.contentGap: Medium — entity definition is unclear even on our own pages

summary.recommendedSequence: Execute in rank order. Ranks 1-3 (Pillar consolidations) should be completed before ranks 4-10 (supporting pages).
